## Description

<!-- Required: describe what changed and why. Include user-facing, operator-facing, or agent-facing impact. -->

<!-- This template is a behavioral checkpoint, not just PR formatting. Fill it out intentionally so the branch-scope, review, split, docs, cleanup, and validation decisions are made explicit. -->

## Scope And Branch Review

<!-- Required: describe what work currently exists on this branch and what this PR includes. -->

- [ ] I reviewed all work currently on this branch, including work not originally authored by me.
- [ ] I identified which branch changes belong in this PR, or explained why the whole branch belongs together.
- Branch/work reviewed:
  <!-- Example: commits inspected, files/directories reviewed, or "single-commit docs-only branch." -->
- Included in this PR:
  <!-- Example: all branch changes, or specific commits/files. -->
- Excluded or intentionally left for another PR:
  <!-- Use "None" if everything belongs here. -->

## PR Shape / Split Rationale

<!-- Required: answer affirmatively with a rationale. "Yes, already split..." and "Yes, kept together because..." are both valid. -->

- [ ] I considered whether this work should be split into multiple PRs.
- Rationale:
  <!-- Examples: "Yes, already split from the feature work"; "Yes, kept together because these docs changes are one reviewable ownership pass"; "Yes, split follow-up deployment cleanup into a later PR." -->

## Independent / Subagent Review

<!-- Required: answer one path. Do not get stuck if a subagent was not useful; give the rationale. -->

- [ ] Yes, I called a subagent or independent reviewer and incorporated or recorded the result.
- [ ] Not called because this change was narrow/mechanical, no suitable independent review was available, or the branch had already been reviewed.
- Review notes:
  <!-- Who/what reviewed it, what scope they reviewed, and any findings or rationale for not calling one. -->

## Documentation Updates

<!-- Required for every PR. Use "None required" only with a short reason. -->

- Documentation changed:
  <!-- Files changed and why. -->
- Documentation considered but not changed:
  <!-- Files checked and why no edit was needed. -->
- `docs/documentation-workflow.md` read for docs changes:
  <!-- Yes / N/A with reason. -->

## Superseded Docs / Cleanup

<!-- Required: describe cleanup review, even if no cleanup was needed. -->

- Superseded or historical docs reviewed:
  <!-- Example: docs/history, deploy docs, old plans, related README/AGENTS guidance. -->
- Cleanup performed:
  <!-- Updated, summarized, marked superseded, linked, or "None." -->
- Wording/change rationale:
  <!-- Justify why wording was added, moved, trimmed, or left alone. -->

## Validation

<!-- Required: list commands/checks. Use "Not run" with rationale when appropriate. -->

- [ ] Tests or checks run:
  <!-- Example: npm run lint; npm run test:run; git diff --check; docs drift scan. -->
- [ ] If checks were skipped, I explained why:
  <!-- Example: docs-only change; no executable path changed. -->

## Risk / Rollback

<!-- Required: summarize risk and how to undo if needed. -->

- Risk level:
  <!-- Low / Medium / High, with reason. -->
- Rollback:
  <!-- Example: revert this PR; revert a specific commit; restore previous doc wording. -->
